title: Overview
description: What is Ultimate Toolkit and what can it do
content:
Getting Started

Welcome to Ultimate Toolkit! This guide will help you get up and running quickly.

What is Ultimate Toolkit?

Ultimate Toolkit is an open-source Windows utility toolkit built with Rust and React. It provides a growing ecosystem of plugins for productivity tasks like OCR text extraction, AI chat, and more.

Prerequisites

- Windows 10 or 11
- For pre-built installers: just download and run
- For building from source: Node.js 18+ and Rust

title: Quick Start
description: Download, install, and run in 5 minutes
content:
Quick Start

Get Ultimate Toolkit running in 5 minutes.

Download

Grab the latest installer from the Releases https://github.com/Ege-BULUT/ultimate_toolkit_by_egebulut/releases page and run it.

First Run

1. Launch Ultimate Toolkit
2. The Welcome screen introduces the main features
3. Browse plugins from the sidebar
4. Toggle plugins on/off from the Plugin Manager

Next Steps

- Configure AI providers in the AI Chat settings
- Install additional OCR languages
- Explore the plugin system

title: Installation
content:
Installation

Pre-built Installer (Recommended)

1. Download the latest -setup.exe from GitHub Releases https://github.com/Ege-BULUT/ultimate_toolkit_by_egebulut/releases
2. Run the installer (no admin required)
3. Launch from the Start Menu or desktop shortcut

Building from Source

See Building from Source building-from-source for instructions.

title: Building from Source
description: Compile the app yourself with Rust and Node.js
content:
Building from Source

Prerequisites

- Rust https://rustup.rs (stable toolchain)
- Node.js 18+
- Git

Steps

`bash
git clone https://github.com/egebulut/ultimate_toolkit_by_egebulut.git
cd ultimate_toolkit_by_egebulut
npm install
npm run tauri:dev
`

For a production build:

`bash
npm run tauri:build
`

title: Overview
content:
OCR Plugin

Extract text from images, screenshots, and screen regions.

Features

- 15+ languages with Tesseract OCR
- Clipboard paste — recognize text from your clipboard
- Region capture — select any area on screen
- Multi-engine support (Tesseract, PaddleOCR, TrOCR)

How to Use

1. Enable the OCR plugin from the Plugin Manager
2. Open the OCR config page
3. Select your language and engine
4. Paste an image or use screen capture

title: Custom OCR Engines
description: Add PaddleOCR, TrOCR, and other engines
content:
Custom OCR Engines

By default, Ultimate Toolkit uses Tesseract OCR. You can add custom engines.

PaddleOCR

Excellent for Chinese, Japanese, and Korean text.

`bash
pip install paddleocr
`

TrOCR

Microsoft's Transformer-based OCR for highest accuracy.

Use via HuggingFace inference API or run locally with pip install transformers torch.

title: Supported Languages
description: Language packs and how to install them
content:
Supported Languages

Pre-installed

- English (eng)

Available Languages

Additional language packs can be downloaded from the OCR config page:

- Chinese Simplified (chi_sim)
- Chinese Traditional (chi_tra)
- French (fra)
- German (deu)
- Japanese (jpn)
- Korean (kor)
- Spanish (spa)
- Turkish (tur)
- And more...

Manual Installation

Language data is stored at %APPDATA%/ultimate-toolkit/tessdata/. Download .traineddata files from tesseract-ocr/tessdata_fast https://github.com/tesseract-ocr/tes

title: Overview
description: Chat with leading AI models from one window
content:
AI Chat Plugin

Chat with leading AI models from a single floating window.

Supported Providers

- Ollama (local, auto-detected)
- OpenAI (GPT-4o, GPT-4, GPT-3.5)
- Anthropic (Claude 3 Opus, Sonnet, Haiku)
- Google Gemini (Gemini 2.0 Flash, 1.5 Pro)
- OpenRouter (200+ models)
- DeepSeek (DeepSeek Chat, Coder)
- HuggingFace (Llama, Mixtral)

Features

- Multi-turn conversations with persistence
- Markdown rendering in responses
- System prompt customization
- Floating window for quick access

title: Provider Setup
description: Configure OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, and more
content:
Provider Setup

Ollama (Local, Free)

1. Download from ollama.com https://ollama.com
2. Run: ollama pull llama3.2
3. The app auto-detects Ollama and lists available models

OpenAI

1. Go to platform.openai.com/api-keys https://platform.openai.com/api-keys
2. Create a new API key
3. Paste it in the AI Chat config

Anthropic

1. Go to console.anthropic.com/keys https://console.anthropic.com/keys
2. Create an API key (starts with sk-ant-)
3. Paste it in the config

Other Providers

Google Gemini, O

title: Configuration
content:
Configuration

Model Selection

Each provider offers multiple models. Select your preferred model from the dropdown.

System Prompt

Customize the system prompt to set the behavior of the AI.

Conversation History

Chat history is saved automatically to localStorage. You can clear it from the chat window.

Floating Window

Enable the floating UI from the plugin config to access AI Chat from anywhere.

title: Overview
content:
Plugin System

Ultimate Toolkit is built around a modular plugin architecture. Every tool you see in the app is a plugin, and you can create your own.

Architecture

`
Plugin System
├── PluginDefinition     # Metadata (id, name, icon, version)
├── PluginBase           # Lifecycle (onActivate, onDeactivate)
├── PluginRegistry       # Global registry of all plugins
├── FloatingWindow       # Overlay window support
└── PluginConfig         # Per-plugin settings page
`

Plugin Lifecycle

1. Registra

title: Creating Plugins
content:
Creating Plugins

Anyone can build a plugin for Ultimate Toolkit. This guide walks you through creating a Hello World plugin.

Step 1: Create the Files

Create src/plugins/hello/index.tsx:

`typescript
import { PluginBase } from "../core/PluginBase";
import type { PluginDefinition } from "../../types";

const definition: PluginDefinition = {
  id: "hello",
  name: "Hello World",
  description: "A simple example plugin",
  icon: "hand",
  version: "0.1.0",
  author: "You",
  hasFloatingUI: false,

title: Plugin API Reference
description: Full API documentation for plugin developers

title: Overview
content:
Development

Contributions are welcome! This guide covers the development workflow.

Branch Strategy

  Branch   Purpose  
   
  main   Latest stable release  
  DEV   Active development  
  feat/*   Feature branches  

Project Structure

`
├── src/              # React frontend
├── src-tauri/        # Rust backend
├── ultimate_toolkit_web/  # Landing page (Vercel)
`

title: Architecture
description: Project structure, Rust backend, React frontend
content:
Architecture

Overview

Ultimate Toolkit uses a hybrid architecture:

- Frontend: React + TypeScript + Vite
- Backend: Rust + Tauri v2
- Desktop: Native Windows app via WebView2

Plugin System

Plugins are self-contained modules that register with PluginRegistry. Each plugin extends PluginBase and implements lifecycle methods.

Communication

- Frontend to Rust: Tauri invoke() commands
- Cross-plugin: EventBus
- Persistence: localStorage (frontend), filesystem (Rust)

title: Contributing
description: How to submit changes, report bugs, and more
content:
Contributing

Getting Started

1. Fork the repository
2. Create a feature branch from DEV
3. Make your changes
4. Run tests: npm test
5. Submit a pull request

Code Style

- TypeScript with strict mode
- ESLint for formatting
- Follow existing patterns in the codebase

Testing

We use Vitest for frontend tests. Run all tests with npm test.

Pull Request Process

1. Update documentation if needed
2. Ensure CI passes
3. Request review from maintainers

title: Overview
description: All available settings at a glance
content:
Settings

Configure Ultimate Toolkit to your preferences.

Available Settings

  Setting   Description  
   
  Theme   Dark, Light, or System  
  Auto-update   Check for updates on startup  
  Plugin states   Enable/disable individual plugins

title: Theme & Appearance
content:
Theme & Appearance

Theme Options

- Light: Clean, bright interface
- Dark: Easy on the eyes in low light
- System: Follows your Windows theme setting

The theme applies to all plugin config pages and floating windows.

title: Updates
content:
Updates

Auto-Update

Ultimate Toolkit can check for updates automatically on startup. When an update is available, you'll be prompted to download and install it.

Release Channels

Currently all releases are stable. Beta/pre-release channels are planned.

Manual Update

Check for updates from the Settings page, or download the latest release from GitHub.
